free_text: Measurement of nuclide production cross sections for
proton-induced reactions on Mn and Co at 1.3, 2.2, and
3.0 GeV
Al -> Cr -> Al -> Mn -> Al -> Co -> Al
Irradiated for ~100 sec
Measured at different intervals ranging from
0.5 h to 7 d
J-PARC 3 GeV Rapid Cycling Synchrotron
The 1157 keV activity due to 44gSc direct production
is separated from the activity originated from 44mSc
isomeric transition by using the Bateman equation.
Resolution of 1.87 keV (FWHM) at 1332 keV
Corrected for (1) fraction of proton numbers received
by foils (1/0.966 to 1/0.984), (2) gamma
self-absorption (1.00 to 1.01), (3) production induced
by secondary particles (0.684 to 0.9 for 54Mn from
Mn+p as well as 54Mn, 52Fe, 56Co, 57Co, 58Co and 57Ni
from Co+p, 0.9 to 1.0 for other products), (4)
products coming from and escaping to other foils (only
for 7Be from Co+p, 0.953 to 0.968).
Total uncertainty. The uncertainty in the
gamma intensity was propagated when it is
larger than 1%. The other uncertainties
propagated are:
Target foil thickness (<1%)
Proton number determination (2%)
Detector efficiency (1.0-7.0%)
Statistical uncertainty of the
gamma-ray measurement (0.1-9%)
By Hayato Takeshita (2021-12-17): The cooling time for
52gMn activity measurement is enough longer than the
half-life of 52mMn. Hence the measured 52Mn cross
section is considered as the ground state production
cross section.
Data received from Hayato Takeshita
Proofread by Hayato Takeshita (2021-12-20)
On. Received from Hayato Takeshita.
